problem.title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
problem.description: Given the head of a linked list, remove the nth node from the end of the list and return its head.

example.explanation: Remove the 2nd node from the end (node with value 4).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pointers (Optimal) [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Advance the first pointer n+1 steps ahead of the second pointer, then move both together until the first reaches the end. At that point the second pointer sits just before the node to remove, allowing a single-pass O(n) deletion without knowing the list length in advance.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next

def removeNthFromEnd(head: Optional[object], n: int) -> Optional[object]:
    dummy = type('ListNode', (), {'val': 0, 'next': head})()
    first = second = dummy

    # Advance first by n + 1 steps
    for _ in range(n + 1):
        first = first.next

    # Move both until first reaches the end
    while first:
        first = first.next
        second = second.next

    # Remove the target node
    second.next = second.next.next
    return dummy.next

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Two Pass Approach [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: First pass to count nodes, second pass to remove the target node.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

def removeNthFromEnd_two_pass(head: Optional[object], n: int) -> Optional[object]:
    # Count total nodes
    count = 0
    current = head
    while current:
        count += 1
        current = current.next

    position = count - n  # 0-indexed position from start

    if position == 0:
        return head.next

    current = head
    for _ in range(position - 1):
        current = current.next

    current.next = current.next.next
    return head
